entry_rationale: STRONGLY FAVORED: Market is priced at 96% YES (NO ask 5%), indicating the market consensus that the broadcast will include the word/phrase category “Injury / Injured” at least once before the 2026-03-06 05:30 UTC close. Supporting Evidence: Tight spread (1¢) and high activity in the provided dataset (volume 3446, OI 3092) suggest a stable, near-decided pricing signal. Resolution Source: Kalshi resolves based on the game broadcast transcript/recording per market rules (authoritative resolution is Kalshi’s official determination). Risk Checklist: clear resolution source ✓; no known formal appeals/reviews path typical for transcript-based props ✓; stable input (single broadcast event) ✓. Risk Level: MODERATE (selected as the single lowest-risk available candidate after excluding Tier 1 crypto and Tier 3 weather; note this is a Tier 4 word-choice market). Remaining Risks: Potential ambiguity in wording/pronunciation or whether “injury” is said on-air before cutoff. Sources: https://kalshi.com/

STRONGLY FAVORED: The market is priced at YES ask 0.95 (implying ~95% probability) for U-3 unemployment in February being above 4.1%, and it closes shortly before/around the scheduled BLS Employment Situation release window (tight resolution horizon). Supporting Evidence: Resolution should be mechanically determined by the published U-3 unemployment rate in the BLS Employment Situation release (Table A-1), a single numeric datapoint with no discretionary judgment. Resolution Source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) Employment Situation report (U-3 unemployment rate). Risk Checklist: clear resolution source identified ✓; stable inputs (single official statistic) ✓; no formal challenges expected for this statistical release ✓. Risk Level: LOW (selected as lowest-reversal-risk available candidate among non-crypto, non-speaking markets); Remaining Risks: small risk of report release timing/format ambiguities or Kalshi rules nuances, but the underlying statistic is authoritative once published. Sources: https://www.bls.gov/news.release/empsit.nr0.htm ; https://www.bls.gov/cps/

STRONGLY FAVORED: The contract is priced at ~96% YES (yes_ask 96c) that February 2026 nonfarm payrolls will be above -25,000, implying the market expects anything other than a large negative shock. Supporting evidence: BLS’ official release schedule shows the Employment Situation for February 2026 is released at 8:30 a.m. ET on March 6, 2026 (well before this market’s 13:29 UTC close), so resolution should be mechanically tied to that publication. Resolution source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (Employment Situation news release for Feb 2026). Risk checklist: clear resolution source (BLS) ✓; stable inputs (single official print at known time) ✓; no formal appeals/reviews applicable ✓. Risk level: LOW (selected as lowest-risk non-Tier1/2/3 candidate available; note most other 92–96% markets in the dataset are Tier 1 crypto or Tier 3 weather/celebrity). Remaining risks: rare data/reporting anomalies or Kalshi rules nuance about revisions, but the initial release should govern. Sources: https://www.bls.gov/schedule/2026/home.htm ; https://www.bls.gov/news.release/empsit.htm
